review_text: Alright everyone , please read this review carefully as it's 6 july 2025 today and on the 23rd of August 2025 , i will have spent 2 years using these awesome and marvellous earbuds... These have been 2 years of extreme , rigorous and very very careless usage and these earphones are still in prime condition, needless to add I'm an avid consumer of music ,l wear my earbuds almost all day long , I've worn them in the gym , while running , they've seen a lot of sweat , a lot of earwax , most of the days ,i fall asleep while wearing them and get up with them either under them or on the floor, I've been very very merciless with these earbuds , I have not used them ,I have exploited them, after allll of this , they're still in prime condition, the mic on these works extremely well, they charge pretty quickly , the box takes a little longer to get fully charged but it lasts for a long time , call quality, voice quality , everything is awesome , their response to your touch is also very durable and awesome , it happens ever so oftent that I'm in the gym and my phone is drenched in sweat and it's screen starts malfunctioning and as a result I can't take calls , but thanks to these earphones , even after all the sweat and moisture they respond to my touch instantly amd I'm able to take calls , in the gym, while driving, 2 wheelers , 4 wheelers , everything...you know I lost their charging box somewhere , so I purchased another set of the same earphones just for the box and im still using the old earbuds with the new box , I have dropped them quite roughly several times , one or two times I have thrown them away because I was angry due to some reason and they're still going one without an ounce of reduction in quality , this is why I'm calling them marvellous... After such rough and extreme usage , i just can't find any big noticable flaw in them.... But yes , I'll like to add that everything depreciates with time , after 2 years of usage , their batter life isn't as good as when they were new , but the sound quality ,the durability, it's still 90% of how it was when they were new...There may be some people who are extra extra critical and would say they give 4.6 to music , 3. Blah blah blah to sound , to bass , don't bother listening to nerds and wannabe tech gurus , go for it , best in class , marvel of engineering , this is gonna be one of those few purchases that youre gonna be proud of , over and out...

review_text: I am sharing my honest review after using these earbuds for one week. Previously, I used the boAt Nirvana Ion, so my comparison is based on that experience. Pros: The sound quality is good. Battery backup / playback time is also good. The build quality feels solid and premium. Fit perfectly in ears not loose at all. Cons: These earbuds do not have an ear-detection sensor. Because of this, I have to manually pause the music before removing the earbuds from my ears. This feature was available in the Nirvana Ion and was very useful. The Bluetooth connectivity range seems weak. After moving just 4–5 steps away from the phone, the music starts glitching or disconnecting. This was not an issue with the Nirvana Ion—even from two rooms away, it stayed connected.

review_text: OnePlus Nord Buds 3r TWS Earbuds Review (After 1 Week of Use) I’ve been using the OnePlus Nord Buds 3r for about a week now, and I’m really happy with how they’ve performed so far. For the price, these earbuds offer great value and deliver a smooth overall experience. Design and Comfort The design of the Nord Buds 3r is clean, minimal, and stylish. I have the black version, and the matte finish gives it a premium look. The charging case is compact and fits easily in my pocket. The earbuds themselves are lightweight and sit comfortably in the ears. I’ve used them for long sessions—both while listening to music and watching videos—and I haven’t felt any discomfort or pain. The ear tips fit snugly and help with passive noise isolation, which is great when using them in public or noisy environments. Sound Quality The sound quality is surprisingly good for a budget TWS. The bass is punchy, which makes songs sound lively, but it doesn’t overpower the vocals or instruments. Mids and highs are also clear and detailed. Whether I’m listening to music, podcasts, or watching YouTube, the sound is enjoyable. Of course, it’s not at the level of premium earbuds, but for daily use, it’s more than enough. You also get some EQ options through the HeyMelody app, which lets you tweak the sound slightly based on your preference. Battery Life Battery life is one of the strong points of these earbuds. I’ve used them for around 7 to 8 hours on a single charge, and the charging case adds more than 20 extra hours. That’s more than enough for my daily use. The fast charging is also super —a quick 10-minute charge gives me about 2 hours of playback, which is great when I’m in a rush. Connectivity Pairing the Nord Buds 3r with my phone was simple and fast. They use Bluetooth 5.3, which provides a stable connection. I haven’t had any dropouts or lag while watching videos or during voice calls. The auto-connect feature works well too—just open the case and they connect to the last paired device quickly. Calls and Noise Isolation While these earbuds don’t have active noise cancellation (ANC), the in-ear design helps block out some outside noise naturally. I’ve taken a few calls using them, and the microphone quality is decent. People on the other end said they could hear me clearly, even when I was walking outside. Water Resistance These earbuds come with an IPX4 rating, which means they can handle sweat and light splashes. I’ve used them during a light workout, and they performed without any issues. I have the black version, and the matte finish gives it a premium look. The charging case is compact and fits easily in my pocket. The earbuds themselves are lightweight and sit comfortably in the ears. I’ve used them for long sessions—both while listening to music and watching videos—and I haven’t felt any discomfort or pain. The ear tips fit snugly and help with passive noise isolation, which is great when using them in public or noisy environments. Sound Quality The sound quality is surprisingly good for a budget TWS. Calls and Noise Isolation While these earbuds don’t have active noise cancellation (ANC), the in-ear design helps block out some outside noise naturally. I’ve taken a few calls using them, and the microphone quality is decent. People on the other end said they could hear me clearly, even when I was walking outside. They may not have high-end features, but they get the basics right—and that’s what matters most.

review_text: So I've been using the older OnePlus Buds Z2 for a while and was searching for something cheaper with good sound quality. Surprisingly these buds provide a good sound quality than the Buds Z2 and The Soundstage is phenomenal which I didn't expect from a sub 2k Bluetooth Earphones also I'm a Musician with critical ears. One thing that drove me nuts is the choice of using 44.1khz AAC instead of 48khz not that it matters in a bluetooth earphones. I can understand the choice for battery life. It lasts for more than 1 week on a full charge for my usage and earbuds itself easily lasts more than 7 hours with 60% to 70% volume for music listening and calls. Passive noise cancellation is not at all good and you'll be cranking up the volume a lot outdoors so the battery mileage would vary a lot. Didn't have any problems with call quality and microphone indoors a slight degradation will be seen outdoors. The range is not great with the sound breaking at 6 to 8 feet from the buds even in a big room with no interference. The App Has support for changing Predefined EQ ('Serenade' sounds great for me), Game Mode (not recommended for gaming even after turning on game mode) and to change tap behaviour good for the price.

review_text: *Non-Biased review, read full and make your decision* Size ordered: 43 Inches Price for which I bought: 23,262(Credit Card Discount Included) (For fitting of TV you have to contact LG by yourself, the installation is free you have to show the amazon invoice) In-Box: TV, HDMI Cable, Stand Legs, Screws and Some Paper Work Pros: - Really premium finish and build quality - Smooth UI - Best picture quality and colors under 30-35K range(Trust me) - Really good speakers - A reliable and good performing processor Cons: - Not mentioned whether IPS or VA(my unit is IPS I guess) - Nothing else, everything worked fine for me (Will update if I find some issues in future) Review: So the room where the TV is fitted is a living room, not too big not too small. The first impressions of TV were outstanding I mean the price that you are paying for the TV is clearly justified. Upgrading from smaller 32 inches TV the upgrade was kinda huge for me and I was stunned to see the 4K picture quality for first time. Earlier I had a Sony Bravia TV and according to me sony gives the best picture quality a TV can give, but LG changed my mind. In just a few years the quality has improved drastically. The blacks are really good even in an IPS panel. Again I am not sure what panel it is, it's just and estimate. The speakers are clearly more than enough for me. Like on full volume my floor sorts of vibrate, and I don't even have to bother about a sound bar anymore. The SD channels, the quality they provide are really surprising. The upscaling has improved a lot and I can say you can enjoy SD channels on this TV too. And for HD channels, I don't have much to say but "AMAZING". Earlier I was leaning towards other 43 inches TV's of Hisense and Acer, but spending some extra money to get LG was totally worth it. Other alternatives: Samsung Crystal 4k Series but I prefer LG after it's performance. Totally recommend, you can buy this product.

review_text: Magic remote is missing. Not sure anyone from seller or Amazon listening. No body cares - No one bothered to tell why I have not sent the magic remote or when will I receive any. Thinking of returning the TV which is otherwise a very good product. Delivery is awesome! It deserves 5 stars. For 55 UA82, magic remote is part of standard accessories and clearly mentioned in the product description. Checked with LG and they confirmed that all UA82 are packed with magic remote. So where is it? Why wasnt it part of package? Can anyone from Seller/ Amazon please arrange and send the magic remote? Delivery OTP was based on received remote and there was no way remote can be made visible because of opaque packaging. Delivery boy showed TV and the remote in package and didnt allow tearing up remote package! So if the intention was to hide this before delivery Amazon/ Seller are smartly achieving this! At what cost? - Trust of your loyal customer base, if Amazon/ Seller is listening! People reading this - please be careful - Pros: 1. Awesome delivery 2. LG’s quality and assurance of the product 3. Plenty of ports, HDMI, eARC, ethernet, USB 4. Proprietary WebOS - truly different and useful - please give it time to be accustomed if not familiar already 5. Airplay - what a relief! Only iPhone users know - how important this single functionality is! 6. Wifi connection is smooth! It hooked to my dual band router like a breeze! 7. eARC enabled HD audio out to my home theatre! 8. Dolby Atmos surely a great feature add! 9. Gen8 processor, surely a boost, remains under the bonnet though Cons: 1. the product descriptions in Amazon are misleading for this item and in most cases. Amazon is aware of this and clearly lots to improve here. 2. Price is above competition 3. Magic remote and basic stand are missing from the pack. Without these, its actually overpriced. 4. Installation process is simple but not exactly as smooth as google tv. One might feel bit alien at the beginning 5. Airplay and Bluetooth are patchy! Not smooth! Software or some other issue - need to figure out! May be #LG can help! 6. Dolby Atmos - not sure if thats good enough with built in speaker! But with external audio system and eARC - this is easily a Pro! 7. Amazon Freedom sale is not really a sale where you get an item, you have been eying for at an attractive price! Beware, about processing fees, fine print about offer - like upto 4000 - you dont see them until you go deep into offer details! Actually, I ended up paying lot more than what was on offer at the product page 8. Amazon has started monopolising its market positioning - now it puts additional charges such as matket place charge, offer processing fee and dont know what not. May be this is better one way - now we are seeing what all we are paying for! Then whats the value we get for Prime membership! Overall, product is nice to have, price could have been better and seller could have documented product description with clarity and no ambiguity.

review_text: For those who are reading this.. this not entirely a product review. Let me guide you through the entire process of my agony. I ordered this item on 8/10/23 and it cost me 23,262.05/- (after offers on no cost EMI). The item was delivered on 13/10/2023. The delivery was prompt and they informed us that technician from LG will call us for installation. Since no one called, so I called back to LG from the contact info provided in Amazon app and booked an installation request on 14/10/2023. The personnel from LG came on 15/10/2023 and when he checked the item (by simply connecting it to power), it was found to be defective because the screen was not switching ON. I tried to return/replace the item on 15/10/23 itself. But only available option was to book a technician. So, I booked a technician (for 17/10/2023, because that was the nearest date available) and I also called customer care to request replacement and they advised me to wait for technician to check. Technician completed the service by evening of 17th (after again reminding through customer care). After 48 hours (which is the standard waiting period for some reason!!!) there was no response from Amazon regarding return/replace. So, I contacted customer care on 19/10/2023. The customer agent concurred that the technician also agrees that the item is defective and said that only return was possible for that item and that he has initiated the return and that within 48 hours someone will come for pick up. But nothing of the sort was updated in the App. I again waited for 48 hours but no one called or came. Then I again contacted customer care on 21/10/2023, and the agent told me that no return has been initiated. At this point I was fed up. They have blatantly lied to a customer on a recorded call. Then this new agent initiated a return which was scheduled for 24/10/2023. The pick up could not happen on 24th because we were not available at home and I again had to call customer care. The agent rescheduled my pick up to 27/10/2023. Then on 25/10/2023 I received a call from pick up agent that he will arrive in 30mins for pick up of TV. He arrived, checked everything and went off with the item. It should be noted that completion of pick-up was not updated in the app which I specifically asked him to update. I raised the same issue with customer care and they told it will be updated within 24 hrs. Then at 3:00pm in the evening the pick-up guy called me stating that the serial no. was not matching and the he cannot complete return. He had checked everything before pick up and now they are saying that details are not matching. I was naturally furious. I denied to accept it back, but he (pick up guy) started threatening that if I don't take it back I will not get refund. Then today morning (26/10/2023) he re-delivered the item to me and took photos (the usual procedure) and went. He was also not clearly explaining why the return failed. All he is saying is that he is acting as per his supervisors orders. Then, I again called customer care informing them of this issue. The agent cross-checked the serial no. and said that all details are matching and that it is a mistake on the part of delivery agent. Now the pick up is scheduled for tomorrow (27/10/2023). Only God knows how many hurdles I have to cross to get my genuine refund. My only advice to whoever is reading this is, Amazon is a very good platform to purchase products as long as they are working. If you end up receiving a defective piece, you will definitely lose your peace of mind because of their conduct.

review_text: We finally moved from a CRT TV to this after all these years and the difference is drastic! I booked it at ₹39,990 just few hours before the 55 inch UR7500 model went out of stock and didn't came back yet as of 28th of October. The Amazon Pay ICICI Card gave 5% cashback of ₹1,974 further taking the price down. It was delivered within 3 days of booking and the installation guy came on the 2nd day after delivery citing huge rush of workload which is understable during the festive sale period. The technician's work was swift and clean. I took the official LG's 2 years extended warranty from him and told him to book a magic remote for me since it's no where to be found out there. LG doesn't sell their remotes through 3rd party sellers, you have to get it from their service center itself. Since it is a 2023 model the spare remote the technician had didn't register so he booked a new one on our behalf at ₹1,534. I got both the reciepts of the remote and extended warranty on my email so kudos to the transparency of the team. Got the remote after a week, the magic remote is great but kind of useless if you are using a streaming device like Apple TV(see the update below). By adding the cost of extended warranty, soundbar, table and remote we could've got the LG QNED instead but we didn't for a reason. Before booking this TV, I went to an actual LG store to see if the difference between standard backlit LED panel and Nanocell/QNED was as drastic or not, it isn't, atleast I didn't feel it. The contrast, colour depth and brightness is a just slightly better on Nanocell and a tad but more on Qned but it isn't worth paying 55k and 80k for them because this is price range where OLED TV starts showing up like the 48 inch LG OLED A series. The difference between these IPS/VA panel TVs and OLED TVs is very noticeable and cant be compared. Heard that Sony's triluminous tech, Samsung's Neo QLEDs and other mini led TVs with FALD are also quite great but they're very expensive here. In a nutshell, if you want to be under budget just get a standard UHD LED TV, if you can stretch your budget then straight away go for OLED/Mini Led QLED TVs and skip these middleware Nanocell/QNEDs, atleast from LG. After confirming that I got an IPS panel instead of VA, I did all the calibration to make all the shortcomings of IPS presentable to my eye. This depends on each other's personal taste but I was annoyed by the yellowish dull hue most of the modes had, after doing some research I got to know that the color warmth is set to Warm 50 at max to make the picture appear more natural. I selected standard mode to customize everything as per my taste, here's my setup: 1. I set the Color Temperature under White Balance up to "Warm 20" to get reduce the yellow hue a bit but not lose the naturalism. Cold setting although looks vibrant at first, its unnatural, you'll see this when sand will appear slightly blueish to you. You will get use to the Warm whites in a week or two. 2. Had to amp up the contrast to 85, panel brightness at 100, gamma to standard 2.4 to make dark areas appear a bit darker(2.2 is the standard). Auto Dynamic contrast Off. Color Gamut at Native. Color Depth at 55. Color adjustment under "Fine Tune" at medium. 3. Sharpness at 10. Super Resolution, Noise Reduction, MPEG Noise Reduction and Smooth Gradation all at Low. Real Cinema will and should be on if True Motion is off, I like using TruMotion. 4. Regarding TruMotion, it completely depends on your use case and taste. If you select Smooth Movement or set de-judder at max 10 from User-Selection, movements appear too smooth and start looking like a Soap Opera which we call the Soap Opera Effect, artifacts also becomes a bit noticeable. This is needed for high motion content like watching a cricket match but for that there's already a dedicated mode called "Cricket", rather use it while watching any sports related content. I keep de-judder at 3 or sometimes move to 4 or 5 if movies appear too stuttery. If you don't want to get confused just select Natural or Cinema Movement if it appears too smooth to you. Similarly you have to set up these for HDR picture settings when the tv switches to HDR mode. To make colors more vibrant I set the color adjustment under "Fine Tune" at high only for HDR, you can keep that at medium if the picture appears too saturated. Auto Dynamic Contrast at medium. Keep testing and changing settings to tune it up to your taste. My mother who's the primary user of this TV doesn't understand all this but is very happy with it, I'm happy. Thanks LG! Update: Became fed up with the laggy and poorly optimised apps the devs made for WebOS, only Youtube feels smooth because it's developer is Google. The worst offender is the Zee5 App. My mom didn't say but I felt she was struggling with the constant lag and sluggish behavior even with magic remote. The solution? Bought an Apple TV 4k, literally the smoothest UI I've used, even beats my phone! Buy a good streaming device, Fire Stick/Cube is also solid. This leaves the magic remote useless since Apple/Fire remotes are universal, so save the cost and don't buy it, the standard remote to access the TV setting once in a while is enough.

review_text: I recently bought this TV during a sale. The product was delivered on time, but the installation took a bit longer than expected. One thing to note — the box doesn’t include a wall mount or table stand, so you’ll need to mention that to the installation technician. On the very first day of use, I noticed a dead pixel. When I reported it to the LG technician, he said it’s not a big issue unless there are 4–12 dead pixels — only then it qualifies for a replacement. Before purchasing, I watched a lot of YouTube reviews (including Vineet Malhotra sir’s video), and most mentioned that the 43-inch model has an IPS panel. However, mine came with a VA panel. The VA panel better black levels when viewed straight on, but you lose about 20–25% color and contrast at extreme angles. The LG WebOS interface is smooth, though I did notice some minor bugs here and there. The TV comes with a normal IR remote, not the Magic Remote, which needs to be purchased separately. In terms of audio, it has 20W speakers with Dolby support, and I’d say the sound quality is decent for a medium-sized room. However, I experienced a slight audio lag when using Bluetooth earphones after an OS update ✅ Pros Smooth and easy-to-use LG WebOS Natural and rich colors when viewed straight Decent 20W speakers with Dolby support Plastic but decent build quality Good value for money during sale ❌ Cons Dead pixel noticed on day one (not eligible for replacement) VA panel instead of expected IPS panel Normal remote (Magic Remote sold separately) Minor sound lag with Bluetooth earphones

review_text: I got the 50 inch model around 36K. The TV was installed next day of delivery. Overall I am satisfied with the buying and installation experience. Earlier I was using a 43 inch LG FHD TV. So when I have switched to this TV the first reaction was wow, as the images were far more smoother and real to life. So the picture quality is a thumbs up. The TV also looks good with thin bezels. The brightness is more than enough and I watch at below 50% brightness. The only problem is that the colour appears washed out if I change my position from front of the TV to side. This is really annoying, I think it's due to VA panel behavior. My earlier TV was IPS panel which have no such issue. Coming to the web OS, it is neat and clean almost all apps can be installed. But I found software very laggy. I am planning to use my Amazon fire stick which is lightning fast. The experience is made worse by the old generation remote, in fact my 7 year old lg TV remote and this one is almost same and both can be used alternately. They do not provide magic remote in the box due to which navigation through the menu is time-consuming and lack of voice command is a big proble. The magic remote cost 3000/ and need to be purchased separately. However, one good thing is the mobile connectivity. The mobile can be easily paired through Bluetooth and can be used as a magic remote also mobile screen can be seamlessly projected on the TV screen. The setting menu of the TV is not good , I feel that my previous LG TV s menu is far more user friendly. Overall for the price It's a nice TV, but the main cons are viewing angle limitation, slow webos which becomes even difficult to operate due to the absence of magic remote. Sound is average and I don't care much as a I use sound bar. My suggestion is to LG is that do not sell this TV without magic remote in the box as it further exposes the slow Webos. They promise software update, but with a modest processor and 1.5GB RAM , you need a lighter software than a feature loaded software. Regarding other option at this price range is the Samsung only. Sony is of course better but cost more than 10k than LG. The budget brands may be good but I am not sure about after sales service.

review_text: I’ve been using the Soundcore H30i for about a week now, and honestly? These are fire for the price. I didn’t expect this kind of sound around 1.5-2k — the bass hits hard, super punchy and deep, without messing up the vocals or clarity. Perfect for music lovers who want that thumpy experience without distortion. Build and comfort are solid, but being real — if you’re new to over-ear headphones, you might feel a little ear pain or discomfort for the first few days. It’s normal and goes away once your ears adjust. Totally worth it for the sound you’re getting. Battery life is great, dual Bluetooth and AUX are super handy, and the overall audio balance feels way above this segment. If you want affordable headphones that actually feel premium, just go for it. You won’t regret it.

review_text: The headphones offer impressive sound quality for the price. They are lightweight and easy to carry in a bag, making them convenient for everyday use. The battery performance is reliable, a single charge typically lasts 3–4 days with regular use, and the charging time is reasonably fast. However, comfort is a concern during extended use. Wearing them continuously for 3–4 hours, such as during gaming or movie sessions, can cause noticeable ear discomfort and painnon upper layer of ears. I usually need to remove them for a few minutes to relieve the pressure. They are suitable for shorter sessions of 1–2 hours, but prolonged use beyond that may lead to discomfort. If you plan to wear headphones for extended periods, this is an important factor to consider before purchasing. Overall it's a great product, the buttons are responsive and didn't face any issues while connecting to my desktop pc via bluetooth and phone as well, it can be connected to both my device simultaneously.

review_text: I’ve been using the Soundcore H30i for about 3 weeks now, and honestly, for a budget pair of on-ears, these are punching way above their weight class. I was looking for something cheap to toss in my bag for daily use, and these definitely fit the bill. ​The Good Stuff: ​The Battery is Insane: They claim 70 hours, and I actually believe it. I’ve been using them for a few hours every day since they arrived and I still haven't had to plug them in. ​Super Lightweight ​The App is a Game Changer: Usually, cheap headphones just give you what you get. But these connect to the Soundcore app, ​Dual Pairing: It’s actually really smooth. I have them connected to my laptop and my phone at the same time. If a call comes in while I'm watching a video, it switches over without a glitch. ​A Few Things to Keep in Mind: ​They are On-Ear: Just a heads up—the cushions sit on your ears, not around them. If you have sensitive ears or wear glasses, you might need to take a break after a while because they do press down a bit. ​No Noise Cancelling: These don't have ANC. They block out some noise just by covering your ear , and yes the price is cheap so its justified ​Plastic Build: They feel a bit "plasticky." But build quality is even better than a 3000 rupees headphones ​Final Verdict: ​If you’re a student or just need a solid pair of "beater" headphones for commuting and the gym, just get them. For under ₹1,500, you aren't going to find anything else with this much battery life and an actual app to customize the sound. ​Pros: Battery life, App support, Lightweight, Price. Cons: not comfortable at all

review_text: I ordered the phone, but unfortunately received it with a broken screen. I placed a return request immediately. The pickup was scheduled within 3–4 days, but no one came. The app kept showing updates like “unable to contact customer” or “unable to locate address,” which wasn’t true, and then the return was cancelled. I placed the return again, but the same thing happened — no pickup and after 4 days, auto-cancellation. On the third attempt, the pickup person finally came on the last day of the return window, but then refused to take the phone, saying the “quality check failed.” During all these attempts, I had to call customer support many times, as every day passed without action. After the third failed pickup, I was told that returns had already failed multiple times, so an investigation would be needed, and I’d have to wait 5–6 days. After that, i contacted them again. It was only after a fourth return request that the pickup was finally done, after 4 days, the return was completed, and after further 7 days, I got my refund. In total, almost a month was lost in this entire process. About the delivery experience (Delhivery): The same delivery person came twice. He didn’t come to the doorstep, stood at a distance, and spoke in a way that felt quite impolite and mannerless. As this was my first return on Amazon, I was unaware of the process and asked him how it works. Instead of guiding properly, his response felt very unprofessional and rude. I’ve always had good experiences with Amazon’s own delivery staff previously, but this one was disappointing. (Amazon's delivery operations are currently handled by a third-party 'Delhivery' company) Final Review: Though I received a damaged phone, all accessories were present, and the design looked great. Based on the specs, it seemed like a very good phone under ₹10,000. I don’t have any complaints about the product itself. For other customers: If you’re planning to buy electronics or expensive items from Amazon, just be aware — if a return is needed, it may not be as smooth as expected. There may be multiple delays, confusing updates, and even a point where you could be told that the return cannot be processed. Even customer support will not be able to help much. They will only help you reschedule the returns. I don’t think I’ll feel confident buying expensive items from Amazon again unless they are accountable for all aspects of any order, so customer support can truly assist when issues arise.

review_text: I purchased the Haier 325 L 3 Star Frost Free Bottom Mount Double Door Refrigerator and I am very satisfied with its performance. The bottom mount design is extremely convenient as the refrigerator section is at eye level, so there is no need to bend frequently. The “Jhukna Mat” concept truly makes daily use more comfortable. The 14-in-1 Convertible feature is very useful and allows flexible storage as per different needs. Cooling is fast and uniform throughout the refrigerator. Thanks to the Triple Inverter technology, the refrigerator operates very quietly and is energy efficient. The digital display panel is easy to use and adds a premium look. The Graphite Black color looks elegant and modern. The twist ice maker is also very handy. Overall, this refrigerator is perfect for a family — spacious, stylish, and reliable. Considering the features and build quality, it is definitely value for money. Highly recommended. Pros: ✔ Bottom mount design – no bending ✔ Silent operation ✔ Excellent cooling performance ✔ Premium and stylish look ✔ Spacious storage

review_text: Bought this refrigerator online for the first time and I was a bit hesitant, but the Amazon delivery was smooth and hassle-free. The Haier technician came for installation on the same day, which was very convenient. Overall, the online experience was actually better than visiting a store. [At the time of purchase the installation service was showing as cancelled, so I contacted Amazon support immediately and made sure it was scheduled. If you’re buying a costly item like this, keep track of the delivery and service status.] The matt black finish looks very premium and modern compared to regular silver fridges. The touch screen temperature control works well and changing modes is very easy. Anyone in the family can use it without confusion. A few small points: the freezer does not have an LED light, the freezer door has no side storage, and the ground clearance is quite low. If you have a mouse problem at home, you may need a stand to raise the fridge. Overall, a good value buy if you want a bottom-mounted freezer around ₹35k. Similar LG models are much more expensive. This is just a first-day review. Will update if any issues come up later.

review_text: Solid mixer for daily kitchen use I’ve been using this mixer for a few weeks now and overall I’m really satisfied with the performance. The motor feels powerful enough for everyday tasks like grinding spices, making chutneys, and blending smoothies. Even when I grind harder ingredients like dry masalas or soaked lentils, it handles them smoothly without overheating. The jars are sturdy and the lids fit well, so there’s no leakage while blending. I also like that it doesn’t make too much noise compared to other mixers I’ve used before. Cleaning is easy, and the blades still feel sharp after regular use. Design-wise, it looks neat and compact on the kitchen counter. For the price, this mixer offers good quality and reliable performance. Definitely a good buy for daily home cooking.

review_text: The Philips HL7756/00 is a top-tier mixer grinder that perfectly balances power and durability. Its 750W Turbo Motor is a beast, capable of grinding even the toughest ingredients like black gram (dal) for 25 minutes straight without overheating, thanks to its advanced air ventilation system. The set includes three high-quality, leak-proof stainless steel jars (1.5L, 1L, and 0.3L) that cover everything from heavy wet grinding to fine chutney making. The standout feature is the incredible 10-year warranty (available upon product registration), which provides long-term security rarely seen in this category. While the motor is powerful and naturally a bit loud, the efficiency and fine grinding results make it a must-have for daily cooking. Its compact triangular body is also surprisingly easy to clean, making it as practical as it is powerful. This Mixer are Light Weight and Ease To use. There are gives you Lock System Great.

review_text: I purchased the Philips HL7756 mixer grinder trusting the brand name, but it has been a letdown from early on. Major issues: Poor jar and lid quality - The middle jar lid lacks a proper gasket, causing it to pop out during use. Multiple reviews confirm this design flaw makes the jar practically unusable . The plastic quality feels cheap and one reviewer reported the bottom black lid cracking within two months . Motor reliability concerns - Several users motor burning out within weeks of purchase. One reviewer experienced motor failure with sparks and unusual sound after only 50 days of light use . No lid lock safety - The mixer runs even without the lid properly secured, which is both messy and dangerous . Very noisy operation - Sounds like a jet engine starting up, which is annoying for daily morning use . Small jar is too tiny - The chutney jar fills only halfway if you don't want contents splattering everywhere . The positives: The motor when working has good power, and service response was quick when I needed repairs . Verdict: For a product carrying Philips' name, the build quality is shockingly subpar. Between jar issues, motor failures, and missing safety features, I expected much better. Would not recommend.

review_text: Product is really good, the finish looks premium. I was a little scared to order such a thing online without checking it physically. However, all my fear was gone when I opened and taken a look at all the pieces - the finish really looks premium. The weights easily go on the rods and can be fixed quite tight. Since they gave 12 weights - I am able to create a great variety of combinations - which is a big plus for its usability. I did not want to buy the one with dials as I am afraid that they would start to get stuck very soon due to dust (dust is a big problem in the city I live) and there is always a chance that the weights could fall. So I am more confident of the mechanism where I can just put the weights on a rod and tighten them with screw - the gap between the rod and the whole in the weights is enough to offset gathered dust. And such weights should generally not fall unless the screw gets very loose. That is why I chose this product over the more complicated ones.

review_text: Using it for the past 1 month now. Here's my not so short review- Built quality- It is heavy and properly built. The plastic is not of super high quality but I won't complaint considering the price. And no, it's not cheap plastic either. The pads on the ends are solid and won't scratch or skid off. Functionality- You just have to put it in place, check the levels and tighten it. The more tight you put it, the better the stability is. Remember, wooden door frames could crack if too much pressure is applied so I would recommend to either put it on walls or metal door frame. The weight bearing capacity is inversely proportional to the lenght of the bar when extended. I weigh almost 80kgs and have extended the bar to about 1 meter and it is super stable and easily takes my weight. The end pads are made of some kind of plastic/resin and have criss-cross groves that firmly holds the surface. You install/remove it with within minutes, so in exercises like Pull-ups, incline/declie pushups, resistant band exercises etc. Overall full marks for stability and functionality. If you have properly installed it, and kept in mind the length to weight ratio, there's no way you would fall-off. Price- I got it during the sale for 800₹ and I think it's bang for the buck. The kind if quality and finish this product has is usually offered at above 1200-1400₹. So totally worth it. Only con- The foam like thing is super-soft so you might not like the grip in the long-term if you intend to do chin-ups or close-grip pull-ups. And yeah, I do wide arms pull-ups on this, and my hands grip on the metal bar and not the foam portion. Even with the cons- I would strongly recommended this for its price, quality and functionality.

review_text: I don't know how others haven't had this issue (OR) why they didn't point it out (OR) simply, I have bad luck. Got this thrice. Twice in Black, in sizes L and XS. Once in Blue, size XS. Initially got an L cz of the other reviews and pictures. Since my bust is 38", figured that L would be perfect. WRONG. The only difference between L and XS is the length of the top. With different sizes, only the length seams to get shorter- where XS would expose my underboobage if I raised my hands. NO THANK YOU, I am good. Other than that, the fit was the same. Now coming to the fit, each and every top was extremely loose on the waist. I am NOT thin. 38"chest-27"waist. These tops, irrespective of the sizes fit like oversized, broader than my shoulders. Would have fit if I had been 9months pregnant. The material is great. It's not transparent. Very very stretchable. XS fit my chest but the length was too short and THAT loose fit. L fit my chest and the length was perfect but THAT fit at the waist, NO. None of the pictures have that issue. Looked great till my boobs. Then spread like a tent. I don't understand how only I could have gotten defective products, 3 times. Else how did no one ever mention this issue.? It's very stretchable, so I don't think alterations would help. It's sad cz this top would have been so multipurpose.

review_text: I find the WOW Skin Science Brightening Vitamin C Foaming Face Wash quite appealing for its Vitamin C benefits and the effective built-in brush for deep cleansing. While it caters well to diverse skin types, it's essential to consider the maintenance of the brush and potential sensitivity concerns. Overall, it's a promising option for those seeking a brightening and thorough cleansing experience. Pros: - I love the infusion of Vitamin C, known for brightening and revitalizing the skin. - The foaming formula offers a gentle yet thorough cleanse without causing dryness. - The built-in brush enhances the cleansing process, providing deep exfoliation. - It's suitable for all skin types, making it versatile for various skincare needs. Cons: - Maintaining the brush requires regular cleaning for hygiene purposes. - Some users might experience sensitivity due to the exfoliating action of the brush. Do the patch test for your skin type first.

review_text: just received the product yesterday, writing my opinion after just first application. this is like a moisturizer, slightly runny creme like texture, easily gets absorbed in the skin, without leaving any oily residue and the best part no white cast what so ever. it blends in to the skin with minimal effort, almost immediately after application, settles in to non-greasy finish. now coming to the sunscreen filters, it has Butyl Methoxydibenzoylmethane (Avobenzone - the only globally available chemical sunscreen that gives proper UVA protection. It is not photostable so has to be combined with ingredients that help to stabilize it.), Terephthalylidene Dicamphor Sulfonic Acid (A L'Oréal-group exclusive sunscreen agent commonly called Mexoryl SX. As opposed to most sunscreen agents, it is water soluble and protects in the UVA range with a peak absorbance at 345 nm.), Phenylbenzimidazole Sulfonic Acid (A chemical sunscreen agent that gives a strong protection in the UVB (280-320nm) range with a peak protection at 306 nm.), Ethylhexyl Methoxycinnamate (Octinoxate - an old-school chemical sunscreen that absorbs UVB radiation (wavelengths: 280-320 nm). Not photostable and does not protect against UVA.), Ethylhexyl Salicylate (A colorless to light yellowish oily liquid that works as a UVB (280-320nm) sunscreen filter with a peak absorbance at 306 nm. It's not a strong filter in itself, it's always used in combination with other sunscreen agents.), Methylene Bis-Benzotriazolyl Tetramethylbutylphenol (Tinosorb M - a new generation, "hybrid" (between physical and chemical agents) sunscreen that gives broad-spectrum coverage and is highly photostable.). these combined modern and old school filters makes it a good and photo stable sunscreen. along with that it contains antioxidants like Niacinamide, Capsicum Annuum Fruit Extract, Tocopheryl Acetate to safeguard the skin from external pollutants, humectants like Glycerin and Sodium Hyaluronate for hydration, skin soothing agents like Blueberry Extract and Allantoin to calm down skin irritation. it also contains tapioca starch, which is known for absorbing oil, providing a matte finish, and creating a silky texture, so it is suitable for people with oily skin. it does not contain any fragrance, so less likely to irritate sensitive skin. I also did not feel any irritation or burning sensation in my eyes while application. the 125ml pack makes it economic also. 100% recommended for oily skin people.

review_text: My face used to be dried out and tight even when washed with plain water and small white blisters(not pimples) pop up all the time. I learnt that my skin barrier was compromised. I tried using different moisturizers from minimalist, cetaphil and simple but none of them helped, even some of it irritated my skin. After some research I came to know that CeraVe has ceramides which is a molecule found on our layer skin that forms the skin barrier. At first I was skeptikal and thought it's just a another marketing gimmick but decided to try anyway. Within a week I was able to notice difference. My skin was not dry and flaky in the morning and after washing face, IT WORKED!!! In the past, before this dryness issue I used to have pimples, I was able to fix it only by correcting my diet. Reducing sugar intake and cutting oily food so don't just rely on creams and blame it if it doesn't work. If you have very dry and sensitive skin give this a try. Its also odorless and have a nice matt finish.

review_text: This cream has a dense, rich texture right out of the jar A bit silicon-y too. For normal to combination or even mildly dry skin, this cream does a very respectable job. It delivers good hydration and helps maintain your skin barrier without irritation. It doesn't have oils so it's a great fungal acne safe option too. But for my very dry skin? It wasn’t quite enough. Even after two generous layers, it didn’t fully sink in — it sat on top of my skin instead of sealing in moisture. The finish was a bit tacky, and I needed something heavier on top to really lock things down. But it did help me get my skin barrier back up during a funal acne breakout so I do always keep a tube on me in case I need it. It's good for rashes and hives too.
